user_query:
I’m weighing which community blacksmithing class to take this spring for both personal enrichment and to deepen my practical understanding for my medieval craft research. Please help me make a decision using the local files and update my notes accordingly.

Inputs to use:
- Enumerate all class_*.json files under input/classes/ (do not hardcode filenames). The directory currently contains options.
- Load my preferences and constraints from input/prefs.json.
- Update the existing document at input/notes/personal_notes.md.

Decision rules:
1) Apply these hard constraints (exclude any class that violates one or more):
   - start_date >= prefs.start_window (ISO YYYY-MM-DD)
   - tuition_usd <= prefs.budget_max_usd
   - commute_km <= prefs.commute_max_km
   - weekly_hours <= prefs.weekly_hours_max
2) For classes that pass all hard constraints, compute a total_score (lower is better) using:
   total_score = (cost_weight * (tuition_usd / budget_max_usd))
               + (distance_weight * (commute_km / commute_max_km))
               + (hours_weight * (weekly_hours / weekly_hours_max))
               + (schedule_fit_weight if any meeting_days intersect prefs.disliked_days else 0)
   - Use weights from prefs.weights.
   - Round total_score to 3 decimals in outputs.

Deliverables:
A) Create output/decision_scores.json with an array of objects for each discovered class containing:
   - option_id, title
   - passes_hard_constraints (boolean)
   - exclusion_reasons (array of strings; empty if passes)
   - cost_ratio (tuition_usd / budget_max_usd)
   - distance_ratio (commute_km / commute_max_km)
   - hours_ratio (weekly_hours / weekly_hours_max)
   - schedule_has_conflict (boolean)
   - total_score (number rounded to 3 decimals; include only if passes_hard_constraints)
B) Create output/decision_summary.md that:
   - Lists all discovered classes with whether they passed or failed hard constraints (and why if failed).
   - Ranks all passing classes by total_score ascending and clearly marks the top choice.
   - Briefly explains (2–4 sentences) why the top choice fits my constraints and lifestyle priorities based on the provided inputs.
C) Edit input/notes/personal_notes.md in place by replacing the line "Decision pending: [TO DECIDE]" with a concise decision entry that includes: chosen class title, provider, meeting_days, start_date, tuition_usd, commute_km, weekly_hours, and 2–3 bullet points summarizing the rationale (cost, schedule fit, commute, research relevance).
D) Draft an email to inquire about enrolling in the chosen class. Save to output/drafts/enrollment_email.txt with:
   - A first line: To: <contact_email from the chosen class>
   - A second line: Subject: Enrollment inquiry: <class title> (Spring 2026)
   - Body: introduce me as an academic researcher studying medieval blacksmithing; note why this class aligns with my research and schedule; confirm the meeting days listed and ask whether any sessions fall on my disliked day(s) from prefs; ask about required PPE and any recommended pre-reading; close with a polite sign-off (e.g., “Best regards, Dr. [Your Name]”).

Notes:
- Use only the provided local files. Do not assume extra information.
- When listing classes, discover them by inspecting the input/classes directory rather than hardcoding filenames.
- Ensure all specified output paths are created. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
